---
title: "Session timetable"
order: 1
comments: false
---

This is the **nowcasting** course, which runs in the first half of the SISMID week (**Monday to Wednesday midday**).
It is paired with the companion [forecasting course](https://nfidd.github.io/sismid-forecasting/), which runs from Wednesday midday to Friday.
Sam Abbott and Nick Reich teach on both courses.

::: callout-important
## Proposed schedule — for Sam/Nick to confirm

The day boundaries, ordering, and per-session durations below are a **proposed draft**, not yet confirmed.
They are built from the material that currently remains in the course.
The nowcasting content was previously taught on a very compressed schedule (crammed into roughly Day 1 plus the Day 2 morning of the old combined course); now that it owns the first half of the week it has been **decompressed** to give the previously-rushed sections more room to breathe.
Please review and adjust against the real SISMID room hours and breaks.
See [Schedule notes & gaps](#schedule-notes-gaps) at the end for where extra content or exercises would help fill the additional time.
:::

Each session starts with a short introductory talk (the linked slides), followed by interactive practice (the linked notebook), and ends with a short stop-and-review wrap-up.
Indicative day shape: start 09:00, mid-morning break ~10:30, lunch ~12:30–13:30, mid-afternoon break ~15:00, finish ~17:00. Adjust to the venue.

# Day 1 (Monday): Delays and their biases

## Session 0: Introduction and course overview

*Proposed: Monday 09:00–10:00 (60 min)*

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-the-course-and-the-instructors): the course and the instructors (15 min)
-   [Getting started](../getting-started): getting set up, checking installations work (15 min)
-   [Introduction to the course](../sessions/introduction-and-course-overview): how the course is taught and the real-time analysis workflow (10 min)
-   [Motivating the course](../sessions/slides/from-line-list-to-decisions): from an epidemiological line list to informing decisions in real-time (20 min)

## Session 1: Delay distributions

*Proposed: Monday 10:00–12:30 (around a mid-morning break; ~2h15 of contact time)*

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-epidemiological-delays): epidemiological delays and how to represent them with probability distributions (25 min)
-   [Practice](../sessions/delay-distributions): simulating delayed data, then estimating a delay distribution in Stan (90 min)
    -   includes a first, gentle introduction to Stan and Bayesian inference (see also the [Stan reference](../reference/stan))
-   Wrap up and review (15 min)

*Lunch 12:30–13:30*

## Session 2: Biases in delay distributions

*Proposed: Monday 13:30–17:00 (around a mid-afternoon break; ~3h of contact time)*

This is one of the previously most-rushed topics and has been given the most room.

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-biases-in-epidemiological-delays): why real-time delay data are biased (25 min)
-   [Practice — censoring](../sessions/biases-in-delay-distributions): interval censoring, double interval censoring, and correcting for it (75 min)
-   [Practice — right truncation](../sessions/biases-in-delay-distributions): estimating delays in real time with incomplete data (75 min)
-   Wrap up and review (15 min)

# Day 2 (Tuesday): From delays to transmission

## Day 1 review

*Proposed: Tuesday 09:00–09:15 (15 min)* — recap and questions.

## Session 3: Using delay distributions to model the data generating process

*Proposed: Tuesday 09:15–11:15 (around a mid-morning break; ~2h of contact time)*

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/convolutions): using delay distributions at the population level (25 min)
-   [Practice](../sessions/using-delay-distributions-to-model-the-data-generating-process-of-an-epidemic): convolutions, discretisation, observation uncertainty, and estimating an infection time series (80 min)
    -   includes an optional discretisation/double-censoring derivation and a "going further" challenge
-   Wrap up and review (15 min)

## Session 4: $R_t$ estimation and the renewal equation

*Proposed: Tuesday 11:15–12:30 and 13:30–15:30 (across lunch; ~3h of contact time)*

This is the other previously most-rushed topic and has been given extended time, split across lunch.

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-reproduction-number): the time-varying reproduction number (25 min)
-   [Practice — renewal equation](../sessions/R-estimation-and-the-renewal-equation): simulating an epidemic and estimating $R_t$ from infections, then from symptom onsets (75 min)

*Lunch 12:30–13:30*

-   [Practice — improving the model](../sessions/R-estimation-and-the-renewal-equation): the geometric random walk prior for $R_t$ (75 min)
-   Wrap up and review (15 min)

## Session 5: Nowcasting concepts

*Proposed: Tuesday 15:30–17:00 (around the day's end; ~1h30 of contact time)*

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-nowcasting): nowcasting as a right-truncation problem (20 min)
-   [Practice](../sessions/nowcasting): simulating delayed reporting and building up simple nowcasting models, including a geometric random walk extension (60 min)
    -   optional section on what happens when the assumed delay distribution is wrong
-   Wrap up and review (10 min)

# Day 3 (Wednesday morning to midday): Joint nowcasting and the bridge to forecasting

## Day 2 review

*Proposed: Wednesday 09:00–09:15 (15 min)* — recap and questions.

## Session 6: Nowcasting with an unknown reporting delay

*Proposed: Wednesday 09:15–10:45 (~1h30 of contact time)*

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-joint-estimation-of-nowcasting-and-reporting-delays): joint estimation of delays and nowcasts (20 min)
-   [Practice](../sessions/joint-nowcasting): (60 min)
    -   the reporting triangle and joint estimation of delays and nowcasts
    -   optional: joint estimation of delays, nowcasts and the reproduction number
-   Wrap up and review (10 min)

## Session 7: Combining nowcasting and forecasting (bridge)

*Proposed: Wednesday 11:00–12:15 (after a short break; ~1h15 of contact time)*

This session bridges into the companion [forecasting course](https://nfidd.github.io/sismid-forecasting/) that follows from Wednesday midday.

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/forecasting-nowcasting): why combine nowcasting and forecasting? (15 min)
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecasting-nowcasting): connecting nowcasting and forecasting, and evaluating the approaches with proper scoring rules (50 min)
-   Wrap up and review (10 min)

## Session 8: Course summary and discussion

*Proposed: Wednesday 12:15–12:45 (~30 min, before the midday handover to the forecasting course)*

-   [Summary](../sessions/slides/closing) of the nowcasting course (15 min)
-   Final discussion, [further reading](../sessions/end-of-course-summary-and-discussion), and handover to the [forecasting course](https://nfidd.github.io/sismid-forecasting/) (15 min)

# Schedule notes & gaps {#schedule-notes-gaps}

The following notes flag where the decompressed nowcasting-only course leaves thin spots, where extra content would help, and content that no longer makes sense now that the forecasting sessions and their data have been removed.
These are for Sam/Nick to confirm and prioritise.

## Stale forecasting context to resolve

-   **(Resolved) Bridge session referred to forecasting sessions that no longer exist.** `sessions/forecasting-nowcasting.qmd` has been rewritten as a self-contained "taste of forecasting": the intro no longer claims forecasting was covered "in previous sessions", a gentle on-ramp explaining what forecasting is (and how it extends the renewal model) has been added, and the old "Connecting to ILI forecasting" block now reads as a generic note about other surveillance data rather than referring to forecasting sessions. The companion forecasting course is linked for the full treatment.
-   **(Resolved) Reference to dropped data/dependency.** The sentence about data "we downloaded from `epidatr` earlier in the course" has been removed; the session is now self-contained, using the simulated onsets / `infection_times` and the kept `estimate-inf-and-r-rw-forecast.stan` model. No `epidatr` or ILI/`flu_data` is reintroduced.
-   **`gh`/`jsonlite` not declared.** The instructors slide (`introduction-to-the-course-and-the-instructors.qmd`) fetches avatars via `gh` and `jsonlite`, which are not in `DESCRIPTION` (pre-existing). Either add them to Suggests or hard-code the instructor cards so the slide renders offline.
-   **Pre-existing broken self-links in the bridge session.** Several internal links in `sessions/forecasting-nowcasting.qmd` use a `sessions/...` prefix (e.g. `(sessions/joint-nowcasting)`), which resolves incorrectly from within the `sessions/` directory; sibling links elsewhere in the same file use the correct bare form (e.g. `(R-estimation-and-the-renewal-equation)`). This predates the split and is left for a separate tidy-up.

## Thin spots / room to expand into the extra time

-   **Stan / Bayesian onboarding.** With the schedule decompressed, Session 1 could spend longer easing students into Stan, priors, and posterior interpretation, using the existing [Stan reference](../reference/stan) and [Using our Stan models](../reference/using-our-stan-models) pages as guided material rather than optional reading.
-   **Biases (Session 2).** Now the largest block. Good candidates to expand: a worked weekly-vs-daily censoring exercise, a guided exploration of how truncation bias grows during exponential growth, and prior predictive checks for the delay model.
-   **$R_t$ estimation (Session 4).** Given the extra time, the conceptual distinctions already in the notebook (instantaneous vs case reproduction number; stochastic vs deterministic; discrete vs continuous renewal) could each become a short standalone discussion or exercise rather than being skimmed.
-   **Nowcasting (Session 5).** The "what happens if we get the delay distribution wrong?" section is currently optional; with the extra time it could be promoted to a core exercise, motivating the joint model in Session 6.
-   **Joint nowcasting (Session 6).** The combined "$R_t$ + nowcast + delay" section is currently optional; consider making it core, as it ties the whole week together.
-   **Bridge / evaluation (Session 7).** Nowcast evaluation with `scoringutils` (CRPS) is introduced only here. With more time it could be expanded into a fuller, reusable evaluation exercise that previews the forecasting course's scoring material.

## Structural decisions to confirm

-   **Day boundaries and breaks.** The times above assume a 09:00 start, ~12:30 lunch, and a ~17:00 finish with mid-morning/mid-afternoon breaks. Confirm against the actual SISMID timetable.
-   **Where to split Session 4.** It is proposed across lunch; it could instead sit wholly in the afternoon if that flows better.
-   **Wednesday midday handover.** Confirm the nowcasting course closes by ~12:45 so the forecasting course can start after lunch, and decide whether the Session 8 summary should explicitly hand over to the forecasting cohort.
-   **Day-review slots.** Short Day 1 and Day 2 reviews are proposed; drop them if mornings are tight.
-   **Optional/"going further" material.** Several sessions carry optional sections and challenges. With the extra time, decide which to promote to core vs leave as self-study.
